# Testing Performed

---

## Tests Executed

- [ ] Unit Tests <!-- Checks individual small pieces of code (like one single function or math calculation) in complete isolation to make sure the core logic is 100% correct. -->
- [ ] Integration Tests <!-- Checks how different parts of the code talk to each other (like making sure a login button successfully talks to the database and retrieves the correct user account). -->
- [ ] End-to-End Tests <!-- Uses an automated tool to act like a real human user, physically opening a browser, clicking buttons, and walking through the entire app from start to finish. -->
- [ ] Manual Testing <!-- A real human person opens the app and physically clicks around to test the visual look, the layout, and to make sure it feels natural and easy to use. -->
- [ ] Regression Testing <!-- Double-checking the older parts of the app to make sure this new code change did not accidentally break features that used to work perfectly. -->
- [ ] Performance Testing <!-- Testing how the app handles stress, like simulating thousands of users visiting at the exact same time to make sure the servers do not get slow or crash. -->

# Checklist

## Code Quality

- [ ] Code follows project standards
- [ ] Self-review completed
- [ ] No unnecessary files included

## Testing

- [ ] Tests pass locally
- [ ] Existing functionality verified
- [ ] New functionality tested

## Documentation

- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Examples updated if necessary

## Security

- [ ] No secrets committed
- [ ] Security implications reviewed

- [ ] Clear title provided
- [ ] Description completed
